_doc: Ground truth for the lint fixture vault. Every planted defect, one entry per expected finding. The scorer diffs a lint run's findings against this.
_dedup_rule: One underlying fault maps to one finding. A dangling index entry that happens to be written as a wikilink is reported ONCE, as index-drift-dangling, NOT also as broken-wikilink. Reporting the same fix under two headings is noise, not signal.
_orphan_source_rule: Orphan = a page unreachable by navigating the wiki. Inbound links from the append-only ledgers (log.md, journal/*, brag.md) do NOT rescue a page from orphan status: nobody navigates via the log. A page linked only from log.md is still an orphan.
_negative_traps: Case-insensitive links ([[Alpha]] -> alpha.md) and alias/anchor links ([[alpha#Overview|text]] -> alpha.md) resolve to an existing page and must NOT be reported as broken. They are absent from `expected` on purpose; reporting them counts as a false positive.

excluded_types.stale-ticket: mtime-based; not reproducible from a git checkout (fresh clone = fresh mtime)
excluded_types.kit-drift: environment-specific; depends on the real kit checkout, not the fixture
